summary.high_unstructured: This code defines a StockChart React component that renders a responsive line chart of stock closing prices using the Recharts library. It first checks for valid data, then extracts the company name and ticker to display as a heading. The data is formatted for the chart, mapping each entry to a date and closing price. The chart includes X and Y axes, a tooltip, a legend, and a styled line without dots, and is contained within a responsive container for adaptability to different screen sizes.

The response includes the ticker, company name, an array of daily stock data with date, close price, and volume, and the current price.
summaryData.high_structured:
• Defines an async GET route at '/api/stock/:symbol' in an Express app
• Extracts and uppercases the stock symbol from the request parameters
• Determines data source:
  ◦ If mock mode is enabled, calls 'generateMockData', adds a currentPrice field (latest close value), and returns its result as JSON
  ◦ Otherwise, fetches real stock data from Yahoo Finance using the 'chart' method
    ◦ Requests daily interval data starting from 2024-01-01
    ◦ Extracts quotes and company name from the response
• Constructs a response object:
  ◦ Includes ticker, company name (from metadata or symbol), an array of daily data objects (date, close, volume), and now a currentPrice field (last close value)
• Sends the constructed response as JSON to the client

oldSummaryData.high_unstructured: This code defines an asynchronous Express route for GET requests to '/api/stock/:symbol', which retrieves stock data for a specified symbol. It first converts the symbol to uppercase and checks if mock data should be used; if so, it generates and returns mock stock data. If not, it fetches historical daily stock data from Yahoo Finance starting from January 1, 2024, extracts relevant fields (date, close, volume), and constructs a response object including the ticker, company name (from Yahoo Finance metadata if available), and the formatted data array. The response is then sent as JSON to the client.

summary.high_unstructured: This code defines a functional React component named StockChart that visualizes historical stock price data using the Recharts library. The component first checks for valid input data, then extracts and formats the company name, ticker, and price data, converting date strings to a simplified format. It maps the data into a structure suitable for Recharts, displaying the closing price over time as a line chart. The chart includes axes, a tooltip, a legend, and is wrapped in a responsive container, with the company name and ticker shown as a heading above the chart.

summary.high_structured:
• Implements a StockChart React functional component for visualizing stock prices
• Validates input data to ensure it exists and contains entries
• Extracts company name and ticker symbol from the data prop
• Formats date strings to only include the date part (YYYY-MM-DD)
• Maps raw data into an array of objects with 'date' and 'price' keys for Recharts
• Renders a responsive line chart using Recharts components:
  ◦ LineChart displays the price data over time
  ◦ XAxis and YAxis provide labeled axes, with YAxis set to auto domain
  ◦ Tooltip and Legend enhance interactivity and readability
  ◦ Line renders the price trend with a monotone curve and no dots
• Displays the company name and ticker as a heading above the chart
• Ensures the chart is responsive and has appropriate margins

• Defines a StockChart React component that renders a line chart of stock prices
• Formats incoming data to extract date and closing price
• Uses Recharts components such as LineChart, XAxis, YAxis, Tooltip, and Legend
• Displays the company name and ticker above the chart
• Additionally displays the current stock price above the chart
summaryData.low_unstructured: This code defines a React component that renders a line chart of stock prices using the Recharts library, and now also displays the current stock price above the chart.
summaryData.medium_unstructured: The code implements a React component called StockChart, which visualizes stock price data as a line chart using the Recharts library. It formats the input data and displays the company name and ticker above the chart, and now additionally shows the current stock price above the chart. The chart includes axes, a tooltip, and a legend for better readability.
summaryData.high_structured:
• Implements a StockChart React functional component for visualizing stock prices
• Validates input data to ensure it exists and contains entries
• Extracts company name and ticker symbol from the data prop
• Formats date strings to only include the date part (YYYY-MM-DD)
• Maps raw data into an array of objects with 'date' and 'price' keys for Recharts
• Renders a responsive line chart using Recharts components:
  ◦ LineChart displays the price data over time
  ◦ XAxis and YAxis provide labeled axes, with YAxis set to auto domain
  ◦ Tooltip and Legend enhance interactivity and readability
  ◦ Line renders the price trend with a monotone curve and no dots
• Displays the company name and ticker as a heading above the chart
• Additionally, displays the current stock price above the chart
• Ensures the chart is responsive and has appropriate margins

oldSummaryData.high_unstructured: This code defines a functional React component named StockChart that visualizes historical stock price data using the Recharts library. The component first checks for valid input data, then extracts and formats the company name, ticker, and price data, converting date strings to a simplified format. It maps the data into a structure suitable for Recharts, displaying the closing price over time as a line chart. The chart includes axes, a tooltip, a legend, and is wrapped in a responsive container, with the company name and ticker shown as a heading above the chart.

data.selected_code:
<ResponsiveContainer width="100%" height={300}>
        <LineChart
          data={chartData}
          margin={{ top: 10, right: 30, left: 0, bottom: 0 }}
        >
          <XAxis dataKey="date" />
          <YAxis domain={["auto", "auto"]} />
          <Tooltip />
          <Legend />
          <Line type="monotone" dataKey="price" stroke="#8884d8" dot={false} />
        </LineChart>
      </ResponsiveContainer>

summary.high_unstructured: This snippet utilizes the ResponsiveContainer and LineChart components from Recharts to render a responsive line chart that visualizes price data over time. The XAxis is configured to use the 'date' field, while the YAxis automatically adjusts its domain. Interactive features such as Tooltip and Legend are included to enhance user experience. The chart draws a smooth monotone line for the 'price' data, styled with a purple stroke and without displaying individual data point dots.

summary.high_structured:
• Wraps the chart in a ResponsiveContainer set to 100% width and 300px height
• Renders a LineChart with:
  ◦ Data provided by chartData
  ◦ Margins set to top: 10, right: 30, left: 0, bottom: 0
• Configures axes:
  ◦ XAxis uses 'date' as the data key
  ◦ YAxis domain is set to auto-scale
• Adds interactive features:
  ◦ Tooltip displays data details on hover
  ◦ Legend shows chart series information
• Plots a single Line component:
  ◦ Type is 'monotone' for smooth curves
  ◦ Data key is 'price'
  ◦ Stroke color is #8884d8 (purple)
  ◦ Dots are disabled for a cleaner appearance

• Renders a ReferenceLine on the chart
• The y-position is set to the last price in chartData if available
• Line appearance:
  ◦ Red color
  ◦ Dashed stroke (5 5)
• Displays the label 'B & C. Current Price'
• Does not render if chartData is empty
summaryData.low_unstructured: This code adds a red dashed reference line labeled 'B & C. Current Price' to the chart at the latest price value.
summaryData.medium_unstructured: The code renders a ReferenceLine on the chart at the y-value of the most recent price in the data. The line is styled in red with a dashed pattern and labeled as 'B & C. Current Price'. If there is no data, the line is not rendered.
summaryData.high_structured:
• Adds a ReferenceLine component to the chart
• Determines the y-position:
  ◦ If chartData has entries, uses the last item's price
  ◦ If chartData is empty, sets y to null (line not rendered)
• Styles the line:
  ◦ Red color (stroke="red")
  ◦ Dashed pattern (strokeDasharray="5 5")
• Labels the line as 'B & C. Current Price'
• Visually marks the latest price point on the chart for user reference

oldSummaryData.high_unstructured: This snippet adds a ReferenceLine to the chart, positioning it at the y-value corresponding to the most recent closing price in the chartData array. The line is styled with a red color and a dashed pattern (strokeDasharray '5 5') for visual distinction. It is labeled 'Current Price' to indicate its significance. If the chartData array is empty, the y-value is set to null, preventing the line from rendering.
oldSummaryData.low_structured:
• Adds a reference line to the chart
• Line is red and dashed
• Marks the current price

metadata.originalCode:
<ReferenceLine
            y={
              chartData.length > 0
                ? chartData[chartData.length - 1].price
                : null
            }
            stroke="red"
            strokeDasharray="5 5"
            label="B & C. Current Price"
          />
